assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following features are NOT typical of kouros figures?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
**Art historians do not believe that Kouros figures wore ornate jewelry. **

![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/bd5bfeb09bd4a248907ba5dc0ab363b8.jpg)

assessment_items.question_content:
**The *New York Kouros* shows the influence of [[☃ dropdown 1]] art.**



![Marble Statue of a kouros (New York Kouros), c. 600–580 B.C.E., Attic, archaic period, Naxian marble, 194.6 x 51.6 cm (The Metropolitan Museum of Art, New York)](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/6269c696488aba7747c22309bc671422.jpg)
hints: **This figure shows the influence of Egyptian and Greek art.**

assessment_items.question_content:
**This is not a portrait of a real man, but an idealized figure, perhaps used as a grave marker.**


![Marble Statue of a kouros (New York Kouros), c. 600–580 B.C.E., Attic, archaic period, Naxian marble, 194.6 x 51.6 cm (The Metropolitan Museum of Art, New York)](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/6269c696488aba7747c22309bc671422.jpg)

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: **This is correct, the figure is not a real, but idealized, and may have been used as a grave marker.**
